items.after:
Add a single-window transcriber surface to the target app.

**`index.html`** ŌĆö load a new sibling `helpers.js` after the
XMLUI standalone bundle so its `window.voice*` exports exist
before XMLUI mounts:

```html
<script src="./xmlui/xmlui-standalone.umd.js"></script>
<script src="./helpers.js"></script>
```

**`helpers.js`** (new, ~120 lines) ŌĆö owns the MediaRecorder
state machine. Exposes:

- `window.voiceToggle(onTranscript)` ŌĆö single entry point.
  - Idle ŌåÆ starts recording: calls `getUserMedia({audio:true})`,
    creates `MediaRecorder` (opus/webm, falls back to default),
    pushes chunks into a local array, returns `"recording"`.
  - Recording ŌåÆ stops: stops the recorder, releases the mic
    stream, returns `"processing"`. When `onstop` fires, POSTs the
    blob as multipart field `file` to
    `http://127.0.0.1:18080/inference`, parses `{ text: "..." }`,
    invokes `onTranscript(text)` (which the XMLUI button passes
    in as `t => transcript.setValue((transcript.value || '') + t)`).
  - On fetch failure or `whisper-server` unreachable,
    `onTranscript("")` is called with `console.warn` ŌĆö silent
    no-op in the UI, error visible in DevTools. (No in-app
    error surface in this MVP; matches the user's stated scope.)

- `window.voicePhase()` ŌĆö returns `"idle" | "recording" |
  "processing"` for read-only UI state queries. (Optional; the
  toggle's return value already drives the button label.)

The helper file is plain `window.foo = function(...) {...}`
declarations (no module syntax, no top-level `await`) so it
loads as a classic script alongside the UMD bundle.

**`Main.xmlui`** ŌĆö replace the empty shell with the
single-input transcriber:

```xml
<App name="Voice" var.phase="idle">
  <AppHeader><Text>Voice</Text></AppHeader>
  <VStack padding="$space-4" gap="$space-3">
    <Button
      label="{phase === 'recording' ? 'Stop' : phase === 'processing' ? 'ProcessingŌĆ”' : 'Record'}"
      enabled="{phase !== 'processing'}"
      onClick="phase = window.voiceToggle(t => { transcript.setValue((transcript.value || '') + t); phase = 'idle' })" />
    <TextArea id="transcript" autoSize="true" minRows="8" placeholder="Click Record and start speakingŌĆ”" />
  </VStack>
</App>
```

The `onClick` is two statements (set `phase` from the return
value, and the appended-transcript callback also resets `phase`
when whisper returns). That's slightly above the "single
function call" target ŌĆö but the alternative (multiple wired
events, an EventListener component, postMessage gymnastics)
adds more surface than the MVP warrants. If it gets noisy in
practice the next iteration can move the orchestration into a
helper.

Alternatives considered:

- **Replicate Bram's two-window split** ŌĆö rejected: this is a
  single-page XMLUI app; there is no parent shell to host the
  recorder.
- **Probe whisper-server health and show a banner when down**
  ŌĆö deferred: the user said the app assumes Bram is running. A
  silent no-op + DevTools warning is the right MVP; explicit
  health UI can land in a follow-up if it matters.
- **[chosen] One helper file, one TextArea, append-on-result**
  ŌĆö matches the user's described UX exactly: turn mic on,
  speak, turn off, see what was said, edit and continue.

Out of scope (intentionally not in this item):

- Whisper-server lifecycle (assumed running by Bram).
- Cursor-aware insertion (text always appends to end; cursor
  position after append is whatever XMLUI's `setValue` chooses).
- Multi-utterance UI (each Record cycle appends; the textarea
  is fully editable between cycles).
- Error surfaces (fetch failure logs to console only).
- Per-utterance separators (helper inserts a single leading
  space if the existing text is non-empty and doesn't end in
  whitespace; otherwise appends raw).
items.before:
`Main.xmlui` is an empty `<App name="Voice">` shell with only an
`<AppHeader><Text>Voice</Text></AppHeader>` inside it. `index.html`
loads `xmlui-standalone.umd.js` and nothing else. There is no
voice/transcribe surface yet.

Bram's voice path (`app/main.js` lines ~907ŌĆō1485 and
`app/__shell/helpers.js` voiceStart/voiceStop) is split across two
windows: the iframe (page-under-development) calls
`window.parent.postMessage({type:"right-pane", kind:"voice-start"})`
and the **parent shell** owns the `MediaRecorder`, calls the
local whisper-server at `http://127.0.0.1:18080/inference`, and
posts the transcript back via `voice-recording-started` /
`voice-into-result` messages.

That split is specific to Bram's two-window architecture. A
standalone XMLUI app in `/Users/jonudell/voice` is one window, so
it needs the MediaRecorder *in the page itself* ŌĆö same
whisper-server contract (the user runs Bram, which starts and
keeps `whisper-server` listening on `:18080`), but no parent-shell
indirection.

Verified design references:

- `MediaRecorder` config (`audio/webm;codecs=opus`, fallback to
  default) ŌĆö `app/main.js:1061`.
- POST `audio/webm` blob as multipart field `file` to
  `http://127.0.0.1:18080/inference` ŌĆö `app/main.js` lines around
  1383ŌĆō1430 (the inference call after `onstop`).
- XMLUI `TextArea` exposes `setValue(string)` and a `value`
  getter; markup can call them via the component id
  (`inputField.setValue('example ')`).
  https://docs.xmlui.org/components/TextArea
- XMLUI attribute handlers should stay a single function call;
  real JS (async, fetch, MediaRecorder) lives on `window` in an
  external helpers script. `.claude/bram-conventions.md` ┬¦"Code
  organization".
